objective: Leela returns to Keating Engineering and tries to pin Mohit down on her final-year timeline.
opposition: The lab feels altered after summer and Mohit keeps speaking in broad strategic language instead of specifics.
turn: Mohit defers her defense question and calls a mandatory afternoon meeting about a new lab direction.
consequence_cost: Leela's assumption that this year will be straightforward is unsettled before the chapter's main blow.

objective: The lab gathers to understand the semester's work structure.
opposition: Mohit imposes secrecy, frames scarcity as rigor, and assigns each student to attack another student's project.
turn: He tells Leela her dissertation approval depends on stress-testing Omar's model.
consequence_cost: Leela accepts a structure that weaponizes peer dependence and begins the story already compromised.

objective: Leela tries to compare notes with June and Omar in the hallway after the meeting.
opposition: Everyone is performing calm while Ben hovers with eager, faculty-pleasing energy.
turn: Omar admits Mohit separately told him not to discuss his assignment details with anyone.
consequence_cost: The lab's ordinary post-meeting debrief culture is broken on the first day.

objective: June hosts the standing dinner and tries to keep the lab's old routine intact.
opposition: Conversation keeps sliding toward omission because each person senses a trap in direct comparison.
turn: Omar mentions a career-making slot Mohit has privately floated, and the room goes momentarily still.
consequence_cost: Suspicion becomes part of fellowship rather than an exception to it.

objective: After cleanup, Leela probes June's reading of Mohit's new plan.
opposition: June hides alarm under jokes, and Leela is afraid to sound naive by naming what she fears.
turn: June reveals Mohit separately warned her that someone in the lab may be sitting on a benchmark weakness.
consequence_cost: Leela understands Mohit is tailoring different stories to different students, but neither woman says the whole thing aloud.

objective: Mohit asks Leela to investigate a supposed repository breach and wants her to feel specially trusted.
opposition: His urgency is manipulative, and Leela cannot tell whether the breach is real or staged.
turn: He hands her old admin credentials and tells her only she is careful enough to handle it.
consequence_cost: Leela is drawn back into service precisely because she still wants to matter to him.

objective: Leela audits the server logs late at night to identify the breach source.
opposition: The logs are messy, the hour is lonely, and what she is finding could destroy her remaining stability.
turn: She discovers that Mohit himself cloned student repositories into a private Ruderal directory.
consequence_cost: Leela chooses concealment over disclosure and turns knowledge into guilt.

objective: Leela tries to lock down compute time and a stable plan for the conference paper.
opposition: June needs the same machines, Mohit keeps reprioritizing tasks, and secrecy makes coordination look suspicious.
turn: Mohit publicly gives Leela priority while privately implying she now owes him a result.
consequence_cost: June loses access she also needs, and Leela receives a poisoned advantage.

objective: Leela meets Mohit to salvage the paper's evaluation design.
opposition: The data remains unstable and Mohit refuses to let the deadline slip.
turn: He tells her to use the narrow slice that makes the model look robust and call the rest future work.
consequence_cost: Leela is offered a polished euphemism for selective reporting.

objective: Leela reruns experiments overnight hoping the stronger result will replicate cleanly.
opposition: The servers keep failing, her body is giving out, and June is texting for help on her own reproducibility issue.
turn: The rerun still does not hold.
consequence_cost: Leela now knows submission will require either honesty or falsification by omission.

objective: At the deadline, Leela decides whether to submit the paper.
opposition: Time is gone, Mohit is pressing, and helping June would cost the only apparent win available to her.
turn: Leela submits with the unreplicated claim and stops replying to June.
consequence_cost: The paper goes out, but Leela has materially damaged June and her own moral position.

objective: June prepares to give her seminar and wants the room to stay on the rails.
opposition: Mohit is overly helpful, Omar is avoiding her eye, and Leela senses an ambush without being able to stop it.
turn: Mohit rearranges the order of discussion under the guise of efficiency.
consequence_cost: The ordinary seminar format becomes visibly loaded with intention.

objective: June presents her benchmark work to the lab and invited faculty.
opposition: Omar's critique has been engineered to hit her in public, and Mohit forces the discussion to widen before she can stabilize.
turn: Omar reveals a flaw that undercuts June's central claim, and Mohit asks Leela to weigh in immediately.
consequence_cost: June is humiliated in front of the only people whose respect currently matters to her.

objective: Leela tries to speak to June after the seminar before the damage calcifies.
opposition: June is furious, Leela is defensive, and nobody can undo what happened in the room.
turn: June tells Leela that one sentence of support would have changed everything and walks away.
consequence_cost: The friendship breaks from strain into active grievance.

objective: Leela and Omar end up at a late-night buffet to make sense of the seminar fallout.
opposition: Both are hungry, ashamed, and still speaking partly for self-protection.
turn: Omar admits Mohit told him the target paper would be anonymized and framed the attack as routine robustness work.
consequence_cost: Omar becomes less monster and more accomplice, which is harder for Leela to manage cleanly.

objective: Outside in the cold, Leela decides how much of her own knowledge to share.
opposition: Full honesty would expose how long she has been sitting on evidence and how compromised she already is.
turn: She tells Omar there are repo irregularities but omits the Ruderal startup folder.
consequence_cost: A tactical alliance forms, weakened from birth by Leela's selective truth.

objective: Leela receives the Research Integrity summons and tries to gauge its seriousness.
opposition: The email is vague enough to be terrifying and precise enough to threaten her defense timeline.
turn: She learns the meeting concerns unauthorized dataset handling tied to old admin credentials.
consequence_cost: The lab conflict becomes legible as formal institutional risk.

objective: Leela meets the compliance officer and tries to avoid formal blame.
opposition: The logs point to her account, and the officer trusts timestamps more than narrative.
turn: Her server privileges are suspended pending review.
consequence_cost: Leela loses technical access and acquires an official record Mohit can later weaponize.

objective: Leela calls Mohit hoping he will intervene or at least reveal his hand.
opposition: Mohit speaks in calm, almost parental reassurance that refuses actual responsibility.
turn: He tells her the suspension may give her useful time to focus and reflect.
consequence_cost: Leela realizes he can injure her while sounding like a mentor.

objective: Leela goes home for Diwali and tries to disappear into family routine.
opposition: Her phone keeps lighting up with lab requests, and her family keeps asking ordinary questions about graduation and timing.
turn: Anita casually asks what exact month the defense will be.
consequence_cost: The visit's warmth becomes inseparable from Leela's inability to answer honestly.

objective: Late in the kitchen, Leela decides whether to tell her mother how unstable things are.
opposition: She cannot bear the mix of pity, practicality, and deserved concern that truth would bring.
turn: She lies that everything is still on schedule and accepts a transfer Anita sends before bed.
consequence_cost: Relief arrives braided with humiliation and class shame.

objective: Leela and June travel to the Baltimore conference determined to behave professionally.
opposition: They are still carrying old hurt, Mohit is already difficult to locate, and the conference makes them feel provincial and overworked.
turn: Mohit skips their prep slot for an off-program meeting.
consequence_cost: His absence becomes suspicious rather than merely rude.

objective: June gives her talk and tries to recover from the seminar damage.
opposition: The audience is cool, the benchmark issue is still shadowing the work, and Leela can help only at the margins.
turn: The talk lands flat and a pointed question reopens the seminar wound in public.
consequence_cost: June's reputation remains unstable, and Leela shares the shame by association.

objective: June and Leela try to find Mohit and understand what he is doing instead of mentoring them.
opposition: Mohit has a plausible schedule excuse ready, and both women are too angry to think clearly.
turn: June finds Ruderal's pitch deck using their unpublished figures.
consequence_cost: Misconduct becomes documentary fact, not just pattern recognition.

objective: Back in June's hotel room, the two women decide what to do with the evidence and with each other.
opposition: Anger, exhaustion, attraction, and years of mutual dependence make the room unstable in more than one way.
turn: They sleep together.
consequence_cost: The alliance gains tenderness and volatility at once, raising the cost of any later betrayal.

objective: Leela and June compare file versions, timestamps, and figure labels to see whether the deck can prove theft.
opposition: Academic ownership norms are muddy, and Mohit can plausibly claim broad control over lab work.
turn: They confirm the deck uses prepublication figures that were never cleared for outside presentation.
consequence_cost: Their anger becomes strategically grounded instead of diffuse.

objective: The two women decide whom to trust and how to move without alerting Mohit.
opposition: Ben keeps appearing near doors, message threads, and coffee lines, offering help nobody requested.
turn: They identify Ruderal as Mohit's private company and realize Ben is functioning as an informal leak back to him.
consequence_cost: Resistance now requires deliberate secrecy inside a space that used to run on informal openness.

objective: The lab gathers for Mohit's spring planning meeting hoping to secure resources.
opposition: Mohit speaks about budget pressure and professional maturity while holding all the distributive power in the room.
turn: He announces there is funding for only one spring travel slot and one priority defense path.
consequence_cost: Scarcity is formalized and made public enough to govern everyone.

objective: Omar tries to finish his paper under the new constraints.
opposition: His visa status makes failure uniquely dangerous, and the clean result is not there.
turn: He tweaks a baseline in a way that flatters his model beyond what the data warrants.
consequence_cost: Omar crosses a line he would once have refused on principle.

objective: Leela sees what Omar has done and must decide whether to confront him.
opposition: Calling it out may destroy him and still not save her; staying silent implicates her.
turn: She says nothing and helps normalize the manipulated result by proceeding as if she saw nothing.
consequence_cost: The lab becomes a place where everyone knows more than they will say.

objective: Omar arrives in panic because his funding letter has not been signed and his visa paperwork is expiring.
opposition: Time is literal here, and Mohit is suddenly unreachable except through assistants and vague replies.
turn: Leela realizes the delay is not clerical noise but leverage.
consequence_cost: Omar's precarity stops being background and becomes immediate plot pressure.

objective: Leela and Omar go to Gloria Fisk hoping the chair can override Mohit's delay.
opposition: Gloria wants to help without creating a departmental scandal and keeps narrowing the problem to process.
turn: She says only Mohit can authorize the required funding statement in time.
consequence_cost: The institution confirms that the mentor's petty power is structurally real.

objective: Leela turns to Ben and the admin portal to get the letter filed anyway.
opposition: Ben wants reassurance, leverage, and future credit before he takes any risk.
turn: Leela ghosts Mohit's language into the document and promises Ben authorship to get the upload done.
consequence_cost: Omar is saved for now, but Leela has used Mohit's methods to beat Mohit at his own game.

objective: June, Leela, and Omar meet in the library to assemble a grievance file.
opposition: The evidence is scattered across machines, memories, and privately hoarded documents.
turn: They discover their contradictory meeting notes fit together into a coherent pattern of deliberate overlap.
consequence_cost: The idea of collective action becomes technically possible.

objective: Working through the night, they test whether the file is strong enough to survive official skepticism.
opposition: Leela is still withholding the cleanest server proof and the full story of her compromised paper.
turn: June notices a timeline gap that only Leela can explain.
consequence_cost: Trust narrows again at the exact point strategy requires widening it.

objective: They prepare to submit the grievance before dawn.
opposition: Leela must choose between full disclosure that harms her and partial disclosure that may fail them all.
turn: She submits a weakened file without the decisive logs.
consequence_cost: The complaint exists, but its missing center will matter later.

objective: Ben confesses that he forwarded the grievance to Mohit and wants the others to tell him how bad it is.
opposition: He is panicking, self-excusing, and genuinely afraid of losing both advisor favor and peer belonging.
turn: Mohit's preemptive email about possible professional misconduct hits inboxes mid-conversation.
consequence_cost: Ben's betrayal becomes irreversible institutional time.

objective: Mohit meets June privately and offers an industry introduction if she distances herself from the grievance.
opposition: June wants out of the room but also wants a future, and Mohit knows exactly which hope to press.
turn: She agrees to the meeting rather than rejecting it on principle.
consequence_cost: June gains an exit ramp and loses moral clarity.

objective: Leela waits for June's answer and tries to keep the alliance alive through messages and hallway encounters.
opposition: June goes silent, Omar is furious, and Ben wants immediate forgiveness without having repaired anything.
turn: June stops responding after taking the meeting.
consequence_cost: The coalition breaks into isolated survival strategies.

objective: Leela prepares for her pre-defense meeting and tries to assemble whatever remains of confidence.
opposition: She has no dependable advocate in the room, and Mohit has been too calm in the days leading up to the meeting.
turn: He arrives with a printed packet she has never seen before.
consequence_cost: Leela understands too late that the meeting has been set as an ambush.

objective: Leela presents to the committee and argues for a defense date.
opposition: Mohit reframes her work as derivative and ethically compromised, leaning on the prior compliance record and contested dataset handling.
turn: The committee postpones the defense indefinitely pending revision and further review.
consequence_cost: Leela loses the future she has organized her twenties around.

objective: After the meeting, Leela tries to leave the building without falling apart in public.
opposition: Her body is finally catching up with months of managed dread.
turn: She bites her cheek bloody in the bathroom and understands there is no procedural fix left inside the old arrangement.
consequence_cost: Rock bottom arrives as bodily fact rather than dramatic speech.

objective: Leela spends a winter day in the laundromat and around the empty campus trying to imagine a version of leaving.
opposition: Rent, family expectation, and personal shame all argue against any choice that looks dramatic.
turn: June texts about interviews in a tone that is practical but emotionally absent.
consequence_cost: Leela sees that waiting for relational repair will produce no strategy at all.

objective: Leela goes to the frozen lake and then to the union lawyer with the evidence archive.
opposition: The archive will hurt her too, and no one can promise it will produce justice instead of wider wreckage.
turn: She encrypts the full file as `holdout_set` and hands over a copy.
consequence_cost: For the first time she chooses exposure without reserving herself a clean alibi.

objective: The former lab members coordinate the timing of evidence release during Haviland's demo day.
opposition: Ben is wavering, June may still refuse public corroboration, and Mohit's event schedule allows very little room for error.
turn: They commit to multiple release paths so no single act of cowardice can bury the evidence.
consequence_cost: The group functions together again, but only in a stripped and tactical form.

objective: Mohit begins his polished industry presentation to sponsors, faculty, and visitors.
opposition: The performance is persuasive, and institutional momentum favors letting it proceed untouched.
turn: Omar sends contradictory project memos and funding emails to the faculty and sponsor list mid-demo.
consequence_cost: Private pattern becomes public contradiction.

objective: As the room destabilizes, Mohit tries to frame the documents as ordinary red-team practice taken out of context.
opposition: His explanation is plausible enough that the moment may still collapse back into containment.
turn: June publicly confirms the stolen figures and dates in the Ruderal deck.
consequence_cost: The accusation gains a witness Mohit cannot dismiss as confused or disloyal in private.

objective: Gloria and the administrators try to move the dispute into a side room and postpone action.
opposition: Sponsor witnesses, union representation, and multiple aligned documents now make delay visibly partisan.
turn: Gloria places Mohit on immediate administrative leave pending investigation.
consequence_cost: Mohit is stopped, but the lab's research pipeline and all dependent futures are shattered with him.

objective: Leela works through the stripped-down dissertation paperwork that remains after the scandal.
opposition: The official process uses bloodless language that leaves out the lived shape of the year.
turn: She submits a reduced dissertation under a provisional arrangement and secures a modest one-year teaching fellowship.
consequence_cost: Survival is possible, but it is smaller and less triumphant than the life she thought she was earning.

objective: The former lab members say their partial goodbyes and decide what to do with the archive.
opposition: Their futures now diverge, and no shared institutional story can hold what happened cleanly.
turn: They agree to keep `holdout_set` encrypted and private as the remainder no official finding can absorb.
consequence_cost: The plural pronoun survives only as a wounded, deliberate choice.
